name: AnnData (Annotated Data for Single-Cell Analysis)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); scverse / Fabian Theis lab

performance.notes: HDF5/Zarr-backed; lazy loading; sparse matrix support

agent_readiness.notes: Annotated data structure for single-cell genomics. import anndata as ad. adata = ad.AnnData(X=count_matrix, obs=cell_metadata, var=gene_metadata). adata.X. adata.obs. adata.var. adata.obsm['X_umap']. adata.obsp['connectivities']. adata.uns['leiden_colors']. adata.layers['counts']. ad.read_h5ad('data.h5ad'). adata.write_h5ad('output.h5ad'). Concatenate: ad.concat([adata1, adata2]). Sparse: scipy.sparse matrix in adata.X. Zarr: adata.write_zarr(). Backed mode for large datasets.

gotchas: In-place vs copy semantics can cause subtle bugs — be explicit with .copy()
Backed mode (file=...) limits operations — not all functions work on backed arrays
Large sparse matrices in X can cause memory issues if densified
obsm/varm key naming conventions are important for downstream tools
best_when: Single-cell genomics data container (scRNA-seq, ATAC-seq, spatial)
Sharing and persisting scRNA-seq datasets in .h5ad format
Foundation for Scanpy, Squidpy, and scverse ecosystem
Large dataset handling via backed mode (HDF5)
summary: AnnData is the annotated data container for single-cell genomics. AnnData(X=matrix, obs=cell_meta, var=gene_meta). adata.obsm['X_umap']. adata.layers['counts']. read_h5ad/write_h5ad. ad.concat(). Zarr backend. BSD-3 by scverse. The standard data structure for Scanpy, Squidpy, and the entire scverse ecosystem. .h5ad is the standard format for sharing scRNA-seq data. Backed mode for datasets too large for RAM.
